faqs.question: Why are silverfish so common in Peekskill homes?
faqs.answer: Peekskill's location along the Hudson River creates higher humidity levels, and many of the area's older homes have basements and crawl spaces where moisture accumulates. Silverfish are attracted to damp environments and feed on paper, glue, and fabrics commonly found in storage areas, making Peekskill properties particularly vulnerable to infestations.
